root_cause: The web service was configured with retry-storm behavior against a degraded pricing dependency: WORKERS=8, MAX_RETRIES=4, and DEP_TIMEOUT_MS=5000 meant each /order request could hold a worker for many seconds while retrying a dependency that was slow and failing every call. The load generator ran at concurrency 16, exceeding the worker pool, so all web workers were occupied in retry loops and /healthz timed out even though the container was running and CPU was mostly idle.
actions_taken: Inspected container status, stats, logs, web environment, pricing logs, and compose configuration. Confirmed pricing was intentionally degraded with 2s latency and fail_every=1, and confirmed web had long timeout/retry settings while the payments neighbor was already bounded. Updated only the web service configuration in docker-compose.yaml to set RETRY_STORM_DISABLED=1, recreated only sreft-web with docker compose up -d web, and verified the new process logged bounded mode with dep_timeout=400ms. Confirmed /healthz returned HTTP 200 quickly and repeatedly under active load; /order now fails fast with HTTP 502 when pricing is degraded instead of exhausting web workers.
postmortem: A degraded downstream dependency was allowed to consume the entire fixed web worker pool because retry and timeout policy was too permissive for the available capacity. The remediation bounded dependency calls and shed load quickly, restoring health-check availability and preventing worker starvation without modifying the downstream service or unrelated containers. Follow-up work should make bounded retries/timeouts the default for web, add alerts on worker-pool saturation and dependency latency, and include retry-storm/load-shedding behavior in production readiness tests.
